results.title: Cursor Competitors & Alternatives (2026) | Product Hunt
results.content:
It’s particularly compelling for rapid feature iteration and prototyping inside an existing codebase, where context awareness in the editor matters and you want the agent to carry momentum. The trade-off is that the more autonomous it is, the more you’ll want to supervise architectural decisions and keep an eye on reliability during longer sessions.

### Best for

Ideal for fast-moving builders who want an autonomous IDE agent for rapid iteration.

### Standout features

 ✓Cascade autopilot for multi-step tasks
 ✓Unified agent + chat workflow
 ✓Strong revert and rollback UX
 ✓Context-aware editing and diffs
 ✓Write mode for generating new files

## Github Copilot

Github CopilotGithub Copilot

Your AI pair programmer

4.8 · 29 reviews

Learn more → [...] 4.7 · 65 reviews

Learn more →

Windsurf’s defining appeal is an IDE agent experience that feels closer to autopilot, especially in Cascade mode. Instead of stopping at suggestions, it can create files, apply diffs, iterate on implementations, and keep improving the result with less manual back-and-forth than many editor assistants.

Where Cursor often shines in tight, interactive editing loops, Windsurf tries to reduce the “approve every step” friction with a unified agent-and-chat flow and strong revert controls. That can make it easier to experiment quickly, back out changes cleanly, and move fast when you’re exploring approaches. [...] Windsurf

Windsurf is geared toward developers who want an agentic workflow where the editor helps implement features, not just suggest lines of code. Its Cascade experience is designed for longer loops—creating or modifying multiple files, iterating on changes, and pushing work forward with less manual glue. [...] ### Best for

Ideal for experienced developers who want an autonomous agent to generate and iterate on features quickly.

### Standout features

 ✓Cascade agent for multi-step implementation
 ✓Prompt-to-files Write Mode workflow

 ✓Iterative changes with minimal manual apply

results.title: Flowly: Knows your world. Answers to you. | Product Hunt
results.content:
New Screen-aware Coach (macOS, opt-in): When you enable it, a native ScreenCaptureKit sidecar shares lossless captures of the window under your cursor with the model alongside audio, so tips can reference what you're actually looking at. ElevenLabs Scribe v2 STT adds [YOU]: / [OTHER]: speaker labels. Dual-Cmd forces a tip mid-session. Notch escalates to always-on-top with a 3-second watchdog.
 New Knowledge Graph overhaul: Floating detail panel, click-through entity relationships, in-app entity deletion (cascades to triples), liquid-glass zoom controls, flicker-free node drag.
 New What's new modal: First launch after auto-update opens release highlights with a hero matching the website's auth screen and a link to the full changelog.
